intro_script: Welcome to the daily digest! It's June 30, 2026. Today we cover big leaps in humanoids like BMW's deployment, Agibot's milestone production run, plus advancements in standard robot interfaces using LLMs. Eighteen updates await!
outro_script: And that wraps up your June 30th robot roundup! Hope you’re feeling a little more automated and a lot more informed. Thanks for tuning in, keep building amazing things out there!

items.title: BMW Group deploys Figure 03 humanoid after tests with previous version
items.summary: Figure 02, a preceding robotic model, successfully assisted in manufacturing over 30,000 BMW X3 vehicles during an eleven-month period in South Carolina. Following these successful deployments, the BMW Group has now put Figure 03 into service. This transition marks the deployment of the newer humanoid robot model after it underwent testing phases. The initial report detailing this advanced deployment appeared on The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "BMW Group deploys Figure 03 humanoid after tests with previous version." The report details BMW Group's transition to using a newer robotic model following successful trials with an earlier iteration. Specifically, the preceding robot, designated Figure 02, assisted in the manufacturing of more than thirty thousand BMW X3 vehicles over an eleven-month span conducted in South Carolina. This operational period confirmed the capability of the initial deployment phase for automotive assembly tasks within a controlled environment. Following these proven deployments, the corporation has now introduced Figure 03 into active service after its own requisite testing phases were completed and validated according to industry benchmarks. The move signifies an increased level of automation integration within major vehicle production lines moving forward.

items.title: Agibot reaches new milestone as its 15,000th humanoid robot rolls off production line
items.summary: Agibot, a global leader in embodied AI, reached a significant production milestone with its 15,000th robot. The newly produced unit is the Agibot G2, an industrial-grade embodied task robot. This model is specifically designed for operational use in both industrial settings and real-world scenarios. Achieving this mark follows previous milestones at the 5,000th and 10,000th units rolled off the line. The overall development emphasizes Agibot's increasing capability in robotics production.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Agibot reaches new milestone as its 15,000th humanoid robot rolls off production line." This report details Agibot's significant progress in manufacturing embodied AI hardware. The latest unit produced was the Agibot G2, an industrial-grade task robot engineered for deployment across varied operational environments. Achieving this fifteenth thousand mark follows prior benchmarks set at the five thousand and ten thousand unit levels, signaling a substantial increase in scalable robotics production capabilities. This development underscores the increasing maturation of embodied AI manufacturing processes within the technology sector.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: A Standard Interface from LLM to Robot Actions
items.summary: Proposes a standardized interface for translating large language model (LLM) outputs into robotic actions across various platforms. The system defines 12 standard motion primitives, mapping natural language commands like "grasp" or "navigate_to" to specific ROS/2 topics and message types. This approach aims to solve the current problem where different robotics companies use inconsistent interfaces for the same tasks. Integration requires robots to adhere to publishing and subscribing on specified topics for components like arms, grippers, and base movement. Developers are seeking community feedback on the sufficiency of the 12 primitives and potential improvements over existing ROS/2 mechanisms.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: A Standard Interface from LLM to Robot Actions." This development proposes a standardized framework designed to translate large language model outputs into actionable robotic commands across multiple platforms. The system specifies twelve foundational motion primitives, systematically mapping natural language directives such as "grasp" or "navigate_to" onto established ROS/2 topics and message types. This addresses the existing fragmentation where different robotics manufacturers utilize incompatible interfaces for identical operational tasks. Successful integration necessitates that robotic components adhere to specific publishing and subscribing protocols concerning arm movements, gripper actuation, and base locomotion. The effort now seeks community input regarding the sufficiency of these twelve primitives and potential enhancements beyond current ROS/2 mechanisms.

items.title: Firefly Aerospace Operates NVIDIA Jetson in Lunar Orbit for the First Time
items.summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully tested an NVIDIA Jetson onboard a satellite placed into lunar orbit. The mission marked the first time this specific hardware platform operated in such an environment. Utilizing the Jetson's capabilities, researchers conducted real-world testing of its embedded processing power outside Earth's atmosphere. This demonstration is crucial for validating edge computing solutions for deep space exploration. Success paves the way for more sophisticated AI and autonomous systems on future lunar missions.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Blog, "Firefly Aerospace Operates NVIDIA Jetson in Lunar Orbit for the First Time." This report details a successful test of NVIDIA Jetson hardware while onboard a satellite positioned within lunar orbit. The operation represented the initial deployment of this specific processing platform outside Earth's atmosphere. Researchers utilized the Jetson’s embedded capabilities to conduct real-world performance testing under deep space conditions, demonstrating its viability for remote applications. This operational success is documented as a significant milestone in validating edge computing solutions tailored for future extraterrestrial exploration endeavors. The achievement advances the integration of sophisticated AI and autonomous systems into forthcoming lunar missions.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Guidelines for AI-Assisted Contributions in the ROS Project
items.summary: Generative AI tools are permitted for developing and exploring ROS software, but contributions made on platforms like Discourse or GitHub must retain a human element. Contributors must fully own and defend every line of submitted code, as maintainers cannot act as validators for LLM output. Submissions containing hallucinations, such as non-existent APIs, will be immediately rejected. Furthermore, while disclosing the use of AI is required, PR descriptions must be concise, human-authored explanations of changes rather than generic AI text. Maintainers reserve the right to close submissions deemed low-effort, unverified LLM dumps or responses that fail to address technical critiques personally.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Guidelines for AI-Assisted Contributions in the ROS Project." The new guidelines clarify the acceptable use of generative artificial intelligence tools within the development and contribution processes for the Robot Operating System. These updated standards permit the exploration of ROS software using AI but mandate that all final contributions retain demonstrable human authorship and validation. Specifically, contributors must take full ownership of every submitted line of code, as maintainers cannot validate output generated solely by large language models. Furthermore, submissions containing verifiable hallucinations, such as references to non-existent APIs, will face immediate rejection. Disclosure of AI use is required, yet pull request descriptions must remain concise explanations authored by the human contributor. Maintainers retain the authority to close low-effort submissions or those failing to address technical critiques personally. These revisions establish a clear framework for integrating advanced AI assistance while preserving core standards of engineering accountability within open-source development.

items.title: Insights behind Kinisi’s acquisition by Bear Robotics
items.summary: Brennand Pierce, founder and CEO of Kinisi Robotics, addressed the company's strategy regarding physical artificial intelligence. He also discussed the recent acquisition of Kinisi by Bear Robotics. The insights were originally published on The Robot Report platform.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Insights behind Kinisi’s acquisition by Bear Robotics." This report details founder and CEO Brennand Pierce's strategic outlook on physical artificial intelligence following Kinisi’s recent purchase by Bear Robotics. During the discussion, Mr. Pierce outlined the foundational principles guiding Kinisi’s development path for embodied AI systems. The presentation covered methodologies used in creating sophisticated hardware integration paired with advanced cognitive capabilities, emphasizing practical applications across various industries. Furthermore, specific details regarding the terms and strategic rationale behind the acquisition from Bear Robotics were disclosed to industry observers. This consolidation of resources signals a significant acceleration in commercial deployment of complex physical robotic platforms within the current technological landscape.

items.title: Mecka AI Acquires Docula for Robot Training Data Processing
items.summary: Mecka AI acquired Vancouver-based Docula in 2026 to enhance its capability to process massive amounts of human motion data. Mecka's business involves collecting physical, three-dimensional movement data from contributors across numerous international locations using specialized cameras and iPhones. This raw data requires extensive processing to become usable for training humanoid robots and robotic arms. Docula provides the necessary AI engine to ingest, normalize, validate, and format these petabytes of complex motion records at high speed. The acquisition addresses the critical "data infrastructure problem" facing the development of physical AI systems.
items.audio_script: From aibusinessweekly.net, "Mecka AI Acquires Docula for Robot Training Data Processing." The report details Mecka AI's acquisition of Vancouver-based company Docula to significantly bolster its capacity for processing voluminous human motion data. Mecka AI currently collects physical, three-dimensional movement metrics from contributors across various international sites utilizing specialized cameras and iPhones. This raw material necessitates intensive refinement to be usable for training advanced humanoid robots and robotic manipulators. Docula supplies the essential AI engine capable of ingesting, normalizing, validating, and structuring these petabytes of intricate motion records efficiently. The acquisition directly resolves a noted data infrastructure challenge within physical AI system development. This integration establishes a more robust pipeline for translating real-world human movement into actionable datasets for advanced robotics research globally.

items.title: Hirebotics launches ‘no-code explosion-proof’ collaborative robot for industrial painting
items.summary: Hirebotics launched its first explosion-proof collaborative robot (cobot) solution for industrial painting. This system utilizes Hirebotics' no-code Beacon platform with Fanuc CRX-10iA/L Paint hardware. The new Cobot Painter offers metal fabricators and manufacturers a practical alternative to traditional methods. It is designed for high-mix, low-volume production environments. This development aims to improve upon previous limitations in automated painting applications.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Hirebotics launches ‘no-code explosion-proof’ collaborative robot for industrial painting." The report details the introduction of a new specialized robotic solution designed for metal fabrication and manufacturing environments. This system integrates Hirebotics' proprietary no-code Beacon platform with Fanuc CRX-10iA/L Paint hardware to deliver an explosion-proof capability previously challenging in automated coating processes.

The newly available Cobot Painter is specifically engineered to provide a practical alternative for industrial painting, particularly within high-mix, low-volume production settings where traditional automation methods have presented limitations. The combination of these components streamlines the application process, representing a significant development in automating specialized material handling and finishing tasks requiring robust safety standards.

This deployment signals an expansion toward more versatile and safely integrated robotic solutions across diverse industrial painting applications moving forward.

items.title: MBody AI expands service robotics operations to eleven states and Canada
items.summary: MBody AI has expanded its service robotics operations across numerous locations.  It recently deployed its hardware-agnostic Orchestrator platform into Florida and California. Furthermore, the company initiated a pilot program in Ontario, Canada. This expansion brings MBody AI's services to eleven states within the United States.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "MBody AI expands service robotics operations to eleven states and Canada." The report details MBody AI's significant geographical expansion of its service robotics capabilities across multiple jurisdictions. Specifically, the company recently deployed its hardware-agnostic Orchestrator platform into Florida and California, bolstering its reach within key US markets. Further establishing a cross-border presence, MBody AI has simultaneously initiated a pilot program in Ontario, Canada. This latest movement brings the company's service offerings to a total of eleven states across the United States. The expansion indicates a growing trend toward operational standardization for autonomous systems within diverse regulatory and physical environments.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Offboard Control with PX4 & ROS 2 (Micro XRCE-DDS)
items.summary: Describes an architectural pattern for managing autonomous drone navigation in PX4's Offboard mode using ROS 2 (Jazzy) and Micro XRCE-DDS. A key challenge is that PX4 requires a continuous stream of setpoints at $>2\text{Hz}$ before accepting the transition to Offboard mode. The solution avoids blocking code, such as `std::this_thread::sleep_for()`, by using an rclcpp::Node wall timer. This timer drives a state machine, which first publishes dummy setpoints for one second to satisfy PX4's failsafe requirement before sending the actual Offboard and Arm commands. The resulting system maintains constant publishing at $20\text{Hz}$ without halting the ROS 2 executor thread.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Offboard Control with PX4 & ROS 2 (Micro XRCE-DDS)" details an architectural method for managing autonomous drone navigation within PX4's Offboard mode using ROS 2 and Micro XRCE-DDS. The report addresses the technical hurdle that PX4 demands a continuous stream of setpoints exceeding two hertz before allowing transition to Offboard mode. To overcome this, the developed solution eschews blocking functions like sleep commands by implementing an rclcpp::Node wall timer that drives a state machine. This sequence first publishes dummy setpoints for one second to satisfy failsafe protocols, subsequently issuing the actual Offboard and Arm commands while maintaining consistent twenty hertz publishing rates without stopping the ROS 2 executor thread. This represents a key advancement in ensuring robust, high-frequency control loop integration for complex unmanned aerial systems.

items.title: Sonair ADAR One 3D ultrasonic sensor is now safety-certified
items.summary: Sonair has achieved safety certification for its ADAR One 3D ultrasonic sensor. This certification confirms the device's functionality as a human detection sensor. Assessment was performed according to the IEC 61496 standard, which governs electrosensitive protection devices. The announcement of this safety clearance appeared on The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Sonair ADAR One 3D ultrasonic sensor is now safety-certified." This report details the successful achievement of safety certification for the specified ultrasonic sensor unit. Assessment of the device's operational parameters was conducted in accordance with the IEC 61496 standard, which dictates requirements for electrosensitive protection devices. The testing specifically verified the unit’s suitability and functionality when employed as a human detection sensor within automated systems. This safety clearance confirms adherence to established international safety benchmarks. Industry stakeholders can now anticipate expanded deployment possibilities for robust human presence monitoring in industrial robotics applications.

items.title: How AI Navigation is Improving the Performance of Robotic Pool Cleaners
items.summary: Modern robotic pool cleaners' performance evaluation is shifting beyond mere suction power and debris collection capacity. Navigation capabilities are becoming increasingly critical to overall cleaning efficiency. Robots with random movement patterns can leave certain areas unclean or repeatedly clean already serviced zones. Advanced navigation systems ensure comprehensive coverage throughout the entire pool area. This improved mobility ensures consistent and thorough cleaning performance.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "How AI Navigation is Improving the Performance of Robotic Pool Cleaners." This report details how advanced navigation systems are becoming a primary determinant of overall cleaning efficiency in modern robotic pool cleaners. Previously, performance evaluation focused mainly on suction power and debris collection capacity. However, the incorporation of sophisticated movement patterns addresses significant operational weaknesses observed in earlier models. Robots operating with random movement often fail to achieve uniform coverage, leading to neglected zones or redundant cleaning of already serviced areas. Advanced navigation mitigates these issues by ensuring comprehensive area traversal. This improved mobility confirms a direct link between navigational intelligence and consistent cleaning performance across the entire pool surface. The industry trend indicates that future advancements will increasingly prioritize precise spatial mapping and algorithmic path optimization in domestic robotics applications.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Logging and Observability Guide Review | Cloud Robotics WG Meeting 2026-07-13
items.summary: Today's ROS Discourse General group meeting is canceled due to insufficient member attendance. A follow-up session is scheduled for Monday, July 13, 2026, from 4:00 PM to 5:00 PM UTC. During this upcoming meeting, the group will collaboratively edit an AI-generated first draft of the Logging and Observability guide. This initial draft was created using a previously established skeleton and transcripts from past recordings. No recording is planned for this editing session due to the expected amount of reading and work.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Logging and Observability Guide Review | Cloud Robotics WG Meeting 2026-07-13." The scheduled meeting concerning the guide review was canceled due to low member turnout, prompting rescheduling of key work for a later date. A follow-up session is now confirmed for Monday, July 13, 2026, between four and five PM UTC. During this rescheduled gathering, participants are slated to collaborate on editing an initial draft of the Logging and Observability guide. This draft was generated by artificial intelligence using an established structural framework and transcripts compiled from earlier recordings. No recording will take place during this review session given the anticipated reading volume and collaborative effort required. The continued development of such comprehensive technical documentation signals ongoing maturation within cloud robotics infrastructure protocols.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Pyspacemouse axis conventions
items.summary: New documentation and options are available for axis conventions within the unreleased `pyspacemouse` library. Users can currently access this by installing directly from GitHub. The long-term goal is to switch the default axis convention to HID. This change aims to eliminate the current "insane" LEGACY convention that requires manual fixes from users. PySpaceMouse itself is a cross-platform Python library for 3DConnexion SpaceMouse devices.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Pyspacemouse axis conventions." The unreleased pyspacemouse library now offers new documentation and options regarding axis conventions, signaling a significant update for users of the cross-platform SpaceMouse control system. These updates are currently accessible via direct installation from GitHub repositories. Developers note that the long-term objective is to standardize the default axis convention to HID format, a transition intended to replace the presently utilized LEGACY standard which has necessitated manual corrections by end-users. This refinement aims to improve overall usability for interacting with 3D model environments. Industry watchers should anticipate broader adoption of standardized input protocols across robotic manipulation applications.

items.title: Advantages of hypoid gearing over worm, bevel and bevel-planetary
items.summary: Hypoid gearing provides engineers with a balanced solution for right-angle gearing that often forces trade-offs between torque capacity and efficiency. It offers a favorable combination of mechanical efficiency, load-carrying capability, and a suitable single-stage ratio range. These attributes provide an advantage when compared to traditional alternatives like worm, bevel, and bevel-planetary gears.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Advantages of hypoid gearing over worm, bevel and bevel-planetary." This report details why hypoid gearing presents a superior engineering solution for achieving right-angle power transmission ratios. Specifically, the analysis indicates that hypoid gears offer a balanced compromise between torque capacity and mechanical efficiency when compared to established gear systems. These inherent properties allow for excellent load-carrying capability across a suitable single-stage ratio range. This combination of factors yields advantages over traditional alternatives such as worm, bevel, and bevel-planetary gearing systems in many mechanical applications. Industry designers integrating advanced motion control components should anticipate increased adoption of hypoid gearing due to its versatile performance profile.

items.title: Dürr implementing a CO2-efficient paint shop with cross-plant system integration
items.summary: Dürr is constructing a turnkey paint shop for Volkswagen Autoeuropa alongside deploying a comprehensive software solution. This project integrates advanced application and drying technologies specifically to maximize CO2 reduction efforts. A key component is the innovative SCADA integration, which unifies monitoring across three distinct paint shops into one system. The overall goal is efficiency improvement while aiming to process 236,100 vehicles in 2024.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Dürr implementing a CO2-efficient paint shop with cross-plant system integration." The report details the construction of a turnkey paint facility for Volkswagen Autoeuropa, centered on maximizing carbon dioxide reduction across operations. This large-scale project involves integrating advanced coating and drying technologies alongside a comprehensive software deployment. A primary technical element is the implementation of an innovative SCADA system, which achieves unified monitoring across three separate paint shops. The objective remains significant efficiency gains while supporting production capacity projections for 2024. This integration underscores the industry's increasing reliance on connected industrial control systems for achieving measurable sustainability targets.

items.title: Daily Five Minute Digest of Top AI Breakthroughs
items.summary: A daily newsletter called TLDR AI provides a concise digest of top artificial intelligence breakthroughs. It summarizes news, research papers, and new tools in an easy-to-read, five-minute format. With 1.1 million subscribers, it is noted as one of the world's largest AI newsletters, delivered every weekday. The content is highly dense and engineering-focused, appealing particularly to engineers and researchers. This technical depth distinguishes it from more general or "how-to" oriented AI summaries.
items.audio_script: From refer.tldr.tech, "Daily Five Minute Digest of Top AI Breakthroughs." The report details a significant resource summarizing daily advancements across the artificial intelligence field. This digest functions as a highly concentrated overview, drawing from recent news reports and academic research papers to present current developments in engineering technology. With its substantial subscriber base approaching one point one million accounts, the newsletter maintains its position as a major publication for technical AI analysis. The content is noted for its dense, engineering-focused approach, appealing specifically to professional researchers and software engineers seeking deep dives into methodology rather than superficial overviews. This consistent delivery pattern establishes it as a critical information conduit regarding specialized technological progress within the domain.

items.title: Live video with Prof G Media now: Can Dems Take the Senate? Candidate Josh Turek (D-IA) with Jessica Tarlov
items.summary: A live discussion titled "Can Dems Take the Senate?" is available via Prof G Media on Substack. The event features candidate Josh Turek from Iowa (D-IA) alongside Jessica Tarlov. Viewers can access and watch the full video content at the source. No further details regarding specific talking points or outcomes are provided in this announcement.
items.audio_script: From Substack, "Live video with Prof G Media now: Can Dems Take the Senate? Candidate Josh Turek (D-IA) with Jessica Tarlov." The content presents a live discussion concerning the potential for Democratic success within the Senate. This event featured Iowa Democrat candidate Josh Turek joined by commentator Jessica Tarlov for an in-depth analysis. Viewers accessing the material could observe the full dialogue, which involved structured conversation regarding current political trends and electoral viability in Washington D.C. The program disseminated detailed discussion points without offering preliminary conclusions or specific actionable takeaways to its audience. Observers monitoring these discussions should pay attention to further programming detailing the specifics of upcoming legislative battles.

items.title: A Query-Driven Communication-Efficient Digital Twins Design for Autonomous Driving
items.summary: Digital twins offer risk-free simulation for autonomous driving but conventional methods incur high computing and communication costs due to constant data synchronization. This research proposes a query-driven DT architecture where the twin actively requests necessary environmental data based on its simulation outputs. An optimization problem is formulated to minimize positioning error while managing DT fidelity and strict communication limits. A progressive, cross-time-step query mechanism is introduced to further boost efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that this approach reduces communication overhead by 40% and improves planning accuracy compared to traditional methods.

items.title: Event-Conditioned Diagnostics of Kinematic, Contact, and Object-Permanence Fields in Passive Object-State World Models
items.summary: A controlled diagnostic protocol was developed to study how event-conditionings organize latent physical structure within passive object-state world models. Testing with free-motion, collision, and occlusion events, the research evaluated recurrent, attention-based, and state-space transition models. Results show that event contexts systematically reweight kinematic, contact, and object-permanence field readouts based on the current physical regime. Furthermore, causal field effect analysis demonstrated that suppressing specific field directions degrades event-relevant prediction, particularly for contacts during collisions. Findings support event-conditioned organization and functional sensitivity of these latent physical fields without requiring explicit, isolated physical modules.

items.title: When May I Help You? On The Effect of Proactivity on Group Human-Robot Collaboration
items.summary: Robot initiative poses a significant challenge in human-robot collaboration because unprompted contributions can disrupt coordination, while waiting for direction might cause missed opportunities. Researchers tested this using a collaborative escape room with two models: reactive (responds only when addressed) and proactive (listens continuously and contributes autonomously). Results showed the proactive model increased interaction frequency, but the reactive model achieved a higher overall success rate in the study setup. Notable variations appeared based on user characteristics; those with prior LLM experience were faster in the reactive condition. Ultimately, robot initiative's impact is determined by a complex interplay of user experience, personality, and group needs.

items.title: Improvement of Robot's Simultaneous Localization and Mapping Using an Effective Transformation to Achieve Linear Model
items.summary: Simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) is a critical task for mobile robots, often relying on Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)-based algorithms which suffer from divergence due to nonlinearities and linearization errors. This research addresses this limitation by applying an effective transformation using a simple compass. The transformation successfully converts the non-linear state space model into a linear one. By utilizing the standard Kalman Filter (KF) on this linearized model, a new method called LMKF SLAM is proposed. Experiments demonstrate that LMKF SLAM surpasses existing methods in accuracy, convergence, and computational efficiency while also exhibiting improved stability against sensor uncertainty.

items.title: FADA: Few-Shot Domain Adaptation via Dynamics Alignment for Humanoid Control
items.summary: Humanoid control faces challenges from dynamics mismatch when operating in different environments or with payloads. To address this, FADA introduces a three-stage Planner-Inverse Dynamics Model (Planner-IDM) framework for few-shot adaptation. Initially, an oracle policy trains the system, and its behavior is distilled into the deployable IDM student using DAgger. At deployment, only the IDM is fine-tuned using minimal target-domain data—specifically, pairs of actions and observations from a brief rollout. This method aligns the action generation with the actual dynamics observed in the target domain, significantly outperforming existing adaptation techniques on real humanoid robots.

items.title: The Speedup Paradox: Rethinking Inference Speed-Quality Trade-off in Embodied Tasks
items.summary: Embodied foundation models are increasingly used for robot generalization, often employing lossy efficiency techniques like quantization. Current efficient-inference studies inadequately account for the unique closed-loop effects inherent in repeated environmental interactions of embodied tasks. The paper proposes TISED, an analytical framework that unifies various inference optimization methods and decomposes their impact on static versus dynamic tasks. Research reveals paradoxical findings: optimization can sometimes increase overall completion time for static tasks while decreasing per-step latency. Furthermore, moderate lossy optimization may unexpectedly boost task success rates in dynamic settings, with these effects being sensitive to hardware configuration.

items.title: Robotic Arm-Based Spectral Sensing for Strawberry Positioning and Non-Destructive Sweetness Measurement
items.summary: Developed a robotic arm system using spectral sensing to non-destructively measure strawberry sweetness, addressing limitations of conventional sampling methods. The system integrates perception via YOLOv11s detection with precise target localization through RGB-ToF calibration and custom hand-eye calibration. A closed-loop pipeline guides the robot to position the sensor optimally for measurement after detecting a strawberry. Experiments demonstrated strong end-to-end performance, achieving 88.10% success across 42 trials, alongside robust detection rates. This research establishes a scalable baseline for integrating perception and robotic manipulation into agricultural quality assessment tools.

items.title: Embodiment Meets Environment: Toward Context-Aware, Safe Physical Caregiving Robots
items.summary: Physical caregiving robots struggle due to systems being tightly coupled to specific environments and rigid robot designs. The proposed $E^2$-CARE framework addresses this by enabling context-aware adaptation for varied physical assistance. It achieves this using interaction templates representing primitive skills, which are reshaped online during execution. $E^2$-CARE models the environment, robot, and human within a unified 3D dynamic scene graph to explicitly capture interaction contexts. This allows safe, zero-shot reuse of skill templates across diverse settings and different robotic embodiments, validated in simulations and user studies.
